query: We trained a logistic regression to predict loan default on 5000 applications. We have race (white, Black, Asian, Hispanic) and sex (male, female) as protected attributes. y_true is the actual default outcome and y_pred_proba is the model score. We're about to ship — audit the model for fairness and tell us what to do.
expected_behavior: Computes or names a multi-metric audit covering at least selection rate (demographic parity) AND TPR (equal opportunity) per group, not a single overall fairness number
Names or computes an intersectional cut across race × sex (not only marginal race or only marginal sex)

Refuses to declare the model 'fair' or 'unfair' from the metrics alone, naming the trade-off (e.g., that equalizing one metric trades against another when base rates differ) and leaving the deployment decision to the user
